subject: Re: wrpCoordinates after M refine
body:
Hi Mingyu,
I’m not sure about the outputs and what version you’re using but at least internally Warp/M uses positions relative to the center of the tomogram in angstroms - I think this is likely what’s in the output but haven’t checked.
It’s unlikely that you have enough signal for temporally resolved poses to be useful unless you’re at 3A or so - if that’s not the case you are likely overfitting these parameters
Cheers,
Alister
Sent from mobile - apologies for brevity
On Oct 17, 2024, at 05:20, Mingyu Jung <jmg0910@snu.ac.kr> wrote:
﻿Hi all,
I have a question about wrpCoordinates value in particles.star file in species folder after M refinement.

4359.67236  4262.32910  1007.64624  4359.67236  4262.32910  1007.64624  4359.67236  4262.32910  1007.64624   77.43541  63.04351    68.49342   77.43541  63.04351    68.49342   77.43541  63.04351    68.49342  1   Position_123_3.tomostar  tczm1sxAEOHlvCpqjkGwUyb06vg
some of my particle coordinates are higher than 4096 which is physically not acceptable. I wondered whether the wrpcoordinates are actual pixel values or not. In addition, I set unbinned tomogram dimension 3712x3712x2000.
Thank you!
Best,
Mingyu
